_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to synthesise our own interpretation of a literary text and explore how others interpret the same text differently.

_source.w: We are learning to synthesise our own interpretation of a literary text and explore how others interpret the same text differently.
y: Synthesising an interpretation requires us to bring together all our analytical skills into a coherent personal reading.
Exploring others' interpretations teaches us that texts are rich enough to sustain multiple valid readings.
This skill is essential for senior literary study and demonstrates intellectual maturity.
eg: We develop a sustained, evidence-based interpretation of a literary text that integrates multiple analytical perspectives.
We compare our interpretation with those of published critics and discuss areas of agreement and difference.
We discuss how different theoretical lenses — feminist, postcolonial, historical — produce different readings of the same text.
We present our interpretation to the class and defend it while remaining open to alternative views.
We write an extended response that synthesises our interpretation with reference to specific textual evidence.
We reflect on how our interpretation evolved through discussion and further analysis.
